bullets.text: Trump tariff policy remains in flux after Supreme Court setback; administration signals continued push, keeping markets and supply chains uncertain.
Risk assets wobble on tariff uncertainty: U.S. futures, dollar and bitcoin fall; Asia largely shrugs off and Korea’s Kospi hits fresh highs.
Importers reportedly still pay tariffs deemed illegal by the Court, implying refund litigation, cash-flow strain, and lingering border-cost pass-through to consumers.
Retail earnings focus on tariff-driven costs and refund/price strategies, signaling near-term margin volatility for big-box and apparel chains.
Private-credit exposure is driving weakness in asset-manager stocks; investors fear opacity, leverage, and refinancing risk could spill into broader private equity.
Gold’s behavior looks more momentum-driven than safe-haven; bitcoin drops below $65k as policy uncertainty reduces risk appetite.
U.S. weighs a targeted Iran strike with potential escalation; officials warn Iran could activate proxies against U.S. targets abroad.
Iran is reportedly preparing for war and regime survival, increasing miscalculation risk and potential disruptions across Gulf security and energy markets.
Russia intensifies attacks ahead of the invasion’s fourth anniversary; commentary suggests Ukraine’s trajectory has crossed a strategic “point of no return.”
Mexican forces kill cartel leader ‘El Mencho,’ potentially triggering succession violence while testing state capacity and U.S.-Mexico security cooperation.
AI funding is increasingly shaping U.S. midterm politics; tech industry lobbying intensifies against social-media bans, raising regulatory and governance stakes.
A 5,000-year-old ice-cave bacterium shows antibiotic resistance, underscoring ancient reservoirs of resistance mechanisms relevant to drug discovery and public health.
New oxygen gel for diabetic wounds could reduce amputations, pointing to potentially large clinical and healthcare-cost benefits if validated in broader trials.
